# Master Tactic Matrix

| # | Pattern | Signal layer | Research description | Primary ethical risk |
|---:|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Clipping networks | Visibility | Many accounts distribute short-form fragments from source content | Paid reach appears organic |
| 2 | Trend simulation | Visibility | Distributed signals create perception of cultural momentum | Culture is staged as discovery |
| 3 | Creator farms | Credibility | Many creators post casual-looking recommendations | Paid advocacy looks independent |
| 4 | Engagement pods | Visibility | Coordinated likes/comments/shares boost reach | Consensus is manufactured |
| 5 | Automated comments | Visibility | Tool-generated comments fill discussion spaces | Comment quality and trust degrade |
| 6 | Reddit astroturfing | Credibility | Promotional content uses community-native formats | Peer discovery is simulated |
| 7 | Fake reviews | Credibility | Reviews do not reflect genuine experience | Social proof is forged |
| 8 | Edited testimonials | Credibility | Negative context removed from review | Review fidelity is altered |
| 9 | Review gating | Credibility | Positive users sent public; negative users diverted | Representativeness is corrupted |
| 10 | Hidden sponsorship | Credibility | Material connection not disclosed | Audience cannot interpret bias |
| 11 | Fake followers/views | Credibility | Fake indicators imply influence | Importance is misrepresented |
| 12 | Local review fraud | Credibility | Fake/incentivized local reviews | Real-world choices redirected |
| 13 | App rating manipulation | Credibility | App reviews/ratings/installs inflated | Store trust is corrupted |
| 14 | Product Hunt upvote gaming | Legitimacy | Launch engagement pressured/coordinated | Badge becomes synthetic proof |
| 15 | GitHub star abuse | Legitimacy | Stars/follows artificially inflated | Developer trust is distorted |
| 16 | SEO scaled content | Visibility | Pages produced for ranking, not users | Search usefulness is polluted |
| 17 | Site reputation abuse | Visibility | Trusted domain used for unrelated low-value content | Borrowed authority exploited |
| 18 | Expired domain abuse | Visibility | Old reputation reused for low-value content | Historical trust repurposed |
| 19 | Fake leads | Conversion | Forms/calls not tied to real buyer intent | Sales pipeline polluted |
| 20 | Invalid traffic | Conversion | Clicks/impressions not from genuine interest | Ad spend wasted |
| 21 | Click flooding | Attribution | False clicks compete for credit | Attribution corrupted |
| 22 | Install hijacking | Attribution | Fraudster intercepts install credit | Wrong partner paid |
| 23 | Cookie stuffing | Attribution | Affiliate credit assigned without causation | Commission theft |
| 24 | Fake referrals | Attribution | Referral incentives exploited without real advocacy | Incentive system gamed |
| 25 | Bot streams | Visibility | Automated listens mimic fans | Royalties misallocated |
| 26 | AI music flooding | Visibility | Synthetic tracks flood catalog | Recommendation and royalty systems strained |
| 27 | AI clones | Credibility | Persona scaled synthetically | Identity and endorsement blur |
| 28 | Deepfake endorsements | Credibility | Person’s likeness used to imply support | Trust borrowed deceptively |
| 29 | Dark patterns | Conversion | Interface steers users against interest | Autonomy undermined |
| 30 | Fake scarcity | Conversion | Urgency or availability fabricated | Pressure replaces informed choice |
| 31 | Social-proof widgets | Conversion | Viewer/customer counters imply demand | Demand cues may be fabricated |
| 32 | Waitlist inflation | Legitimacy | Waitlist size used as traction proof | Interest overstated |
| 33 | ARR inflation | Legitimacy | Revenue metric exaggerated or undefined | Investors/customers misled |
| 34 | Dashboard screenshots | Legitimacy | Metrics shown without definitions/context | Unverifiable proof circulates |
| 35 | Compliance theatre | Legitimacy | Trust/security claims overstate assurance | Risk decisions distorted |
| 36 | Trust badge misuse | Credibility | Logos/certifications imply validation | Borrowed authority misleads |
| 37 | Press-citation laundering | Legitimacy | Minor coverage reframed as broad validation | Visibility becomes credibility |
| 38 | Synthetic personas | Credibility | Fake identities carry narratives | Source independence fabricated |
| 39 | Rage bait | Visibility | Outrage exploited for spread | Emotional intensity mistaken for importance |
| 40 | Poll/survey manipulation | Credibility | Sentiment or consensus signals distorted | Public opinion appears manufactured |
